upstream_note: v2: full 6x6. Top-level cells are the openai-egress row for v1 compatibility; the full grid is under upstreams.<egress>.cells keyed by ingress dialect.
egress_configured: openai openai-responses anthropic gemini cohere bedrock
capability_note: advisory context only (probe-first: every cell is probed regardless): LiteLLM accepts OpenAI ingress into any provider plus native Anthropic/Responses ingress diagonals; other ingress rows are grey by declaration

openai.verdict_note: HTTP 200, passthrough (same dialect, no translation required); round trip to the mock's openai endpoint confirmed

openai-responses.verdict_note: HTTP 200 with a valid openai-responses envelope, but the gateway did not speak the openai dialect to the upstream: the mock received the request on the openai-responses endpoint instead
openai-responses.probe_note: probe failed: HTTP 200; upstream request landed on the openai-responses endpoint, not the openai endpoint (this pairing is not wired to a openai upstream)

anthropic.verdict_note: HTTP 200 with a valid anthropic envelope, but the gateway did not speak the openai dialect to the upstream: the mock received the request on the openai-responses endpoint instead

gemini.verdict_note: HTTP 200, gemini envelope validated; mock received a openai-shaped request on its openai endpoint

hardware: AWS m7g.4xlarge (Graviton3, 16 cores / 64 GB). Gateway-under-test pinned to 4 cores (the comparable basis); mock and load generator on 6 cores each so the mock never bottlenecks the streaming sweep. Ubuntu 24.04. One dedicated box per gateway.
